projects.title: A value-driven framework to model the impact of commercial thinning on timber supply and climate change mitigation potential in BC's interior forests
projects.summary: A current FRESH project focused on commercial thinning, timber supply, and climate change mitigation potential in BC's interior forests.
body: Commercial thinning can shift the timing of harvested volume and may help mitigate mid-term timber supply shortages in forests with unbalanced age class structures.
This project evaluates economically viable commercial thinning treatments and their potential to enhance timber supply while supporting climate change mitigation in BC's interior forests.
The project uses a value-driven modelling framework to connect treatment schedules, timber supply effects, and climate-related outcomes.
outputs: Publications, model outputs, and supporting documents will be linked here as the project record is curated.

projects.title: CCCANDiES
projects.summary: Cumulative effects modelling for climate change, anthropogenic disturbance, natural disturbance, and forest ecosystem services.
body: CCCANDiES stands for Cumulative effects of Climate Change, Anthropogenic and Natural Disturbances on forest Ecosystem Services.
The project develops modelling capacity for understanding how multiple drivers of landscape change interact across forest ecosystems and affect ecosystem-service outcomes.

FRESH uses this project record to connect cumulative-effects modelling, forest landscape simulation, decision support, trainee work, publications, and related software outputs as the public material is reviewed.
outputs: Public reports, modelling outputs, repositories, trainee outputs, and related publications will be linked here as they are cleared for public release.

The record is intentionally concise while reviewed public language, project scope, and output links are assembled.

projects.title: IgniteBC and FlashForest drone seeding microsite machine learning
projects.summary: Applied work on drone seeding, microsite characterization, and machine-learning support for reforestation decisions.
body: This project connects drone seeding, microsite characterization, operations research, artificial intelligence, and machine-learning methods for reforestation decision support in British Columbia.
The work supports next-generation drone seeding workflows by linking field data, remotely sensed imagery, site conditions, deployment planning, and model evaluation.

FRESH is using the project page to track public-facing summaries, modelling outputs, technical reports, software repositories, data products, and image assets as partner-approved material becomes available.
outputs: Partner-approved technical reports, modelling workflows, GitHub repositories, data products, publications, and visual assets will be linked here after review.

projects.title: CLEWs-C2070 NRCan grant
projects.summary: A collaborative NRCan project integrating climate, land, and water into energy modelling for Canada's net-zero commitments.
body: CLEWs-C2070 focuses on innovative integration of climate, land, and water into energy models for addressing Canada's net-zero commitments.
FRESH contributes forest, land-use, modelling, and ecosystem-service expertise to the broader collaborative modelling effort.
This project page will connect the grant record to public reports, model outputs, publications, repositories, and decision-support applications as the reviewed materials become available.
partners: Hisham Zerriffi: UBC Department of Forest Resources Management
outputs: Public reports, publications, model outputs, repositories, and applications will be linked after review.

projects.title: Single-stem snap-and-fly helicopter logging productivity
projects.summary: A Forest Action Lab collaboration studying productivity, cycle-time structure, and operational drivers of single-stem snap-and-fly helicopter logging in coastal British Columbia.
body: This project studies standing-stem snap-and-fly helicopter logging as a specialized forest operations system for selectively extracting high-value stems.

The work quantifies productivity, cycle-time structure, delay occurrence, payload characteristics, and operational drivers using field observations, video analysis, GNSS flight tracking, mapped stem locations, and contractor-provided payload data.

The case study focuses on coastal British Columbia operations using a Kaman K-Max K-1200 and a Sikorsky S-61N, with FRESH contributing modelling, analysis, and research-record support in collaboration with the UBC Forest Action Lab.
people: Omar Mologni: Forest Action Lab paper lead
Dominik Roeser: Forest Action Lab collaborator
partners: UBC Department of Forest Resources Management
outputs: Field observation, video-analysis, GNSS flight-tracking, mapped-stem, and payload datasets
Public paper link will be added after the manuscript is ready to share

projects.title: GNSS-based area-cover validation for whole-tree logging equipment
projects.summary: A Forest Action Lab collaboration evaluating GNSS-based methods for estimating daily area covered by ground-based whole-tree logging equipment.
body: This project evaluates how GNSS recording frequency and post-processing choices affect estimates of daily area covered by ground-based whole-tree logging equipment.

The analysis compares machine-track-based estimates against field-derived validation data from multiple British Columbia logging operations, with attention to spatial error, operational scale, and the cost of collecting high-frequency GNSS data.
The work supports better digital monitoring of forest operations by connecting machine positioning data, UAV photogrammetry, stand and site information, and productivity analysis.
people: Omar Mologni: Forest Action Lab paper lead
Dominik Roeser: Forest Action Lab collaborator
partners: UBC Department of Forest Resources Management
outputs: GNSS machine-track and UAV photogrammetry validation workflow
Public paper link will be added after the manuscript is ready to share

projects.title: WS3
projects.summary: An open-source Python framework for integrated simulation and optimization of forest landscape and wood supply systems.
body: WS3 is the Wood Supply Simulation System, an open-source Python framework for integrated simulation and optimization of forest landscape and wood supply systems.

The framework supports transparent, reproducible forest estate modelling and decision-support workflows, including links to landscape simulation, wood supply analysis, optimization, carbon modelling, and related research applications.
The public project record connects the software repository, technical documentation, citation metadata, examples, and publications as the FRESH software portfolio is curated.
people: Gregory Paradis: maintainer or lead developer
outputs: Example model instances and related decision-support applications

projects.title: FHOPS
projects.summary: Forest Harvesting Operations Planning System software for reproducible forest operations planning experiments.
body: FHOPS is the Forest Harvesting Operations Planning System, an open software project in the FRESH research software portfolio.
The project supports reproducible optimization and benchmarking workflows for forest harvesting operations planning.
The public project record links the software repository, technical documentation, citation metadata, and related publications as those outputs are finalized.
people: Gregory Paradis: maintainer or lead developer

projects.title: FEMIC
projects.summary: A FRESH software stack for compiling forest inventory and yield-curve data for computational experiments.
body: FEMIC is an open software project for compiling inventory and yield-curve data for forest modelling computational experiments.
The project sits inside the FRESH open modelling stack and supports reproducible model-instance preparation for forest estate and cumulative-effects analysis.
The public project record connects the FEMIC repository, technical documentation, software citation, and related public model-instance documentation.
people: Gregory Paradis: maintainer or lead developer

projects.title: Nemora
projects.summary: A Python package for fitting probability density functions to tree stem tally data from forest inventory plots.
body: Nemora streamlines fitting statistical probability density functions to tree stem tally data from forest inventory plots.
The package supports FRESH work on modelling tree diameter distributions, including diameter-distribution fitting papers and related forest inventory analysis workflows.
The public project record links the software repository, technical documentation, citation metadata, and related publications as they are curated.
people: Gregory Paradis: maintainer or lead developer

projects.title: Modelwright
projects.summary: Software for extracting, validating, and reforging spreadsheet models as pure Python code.
body: Modelwright extracts, validates, and reforges spreadsheet models as pure Python code.
The project supports FRESH work on transparent, testable modelling workflows when policy, planning, or calculator-style models start as spreadsheets.
The public project record links the repository and documentation site while examples and related applications are curated.
people: Gregory Paradis: maintainer or lead developer
outputs: Examples and related model conversion workflows

projects.title: FABLE-Pyculator
projects.summary: Notebook controls for working with Modelwright-generated Python versions of FABLE Calculator models.
body: FABLE-Pyculator provides FABLE Calculator-specific notebook controls for Modelwright-generated Python models.
The project connects FRESH software work on spreadsheet-model transformation with land-system and food-system calculator workflows.
The public project record will connect the repository, FABLE white paper context, examples, and any related documentation as the software matures.
people: Gregory Paradis: maintainer or lead developer
outputs: FABLE white paper and related land-system modelling context
